serving.description: 1 serving (25 g powder reconstituted in 200 ml water)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is approximately 25 g of powder (about 3 tablespoons) reconstituted in 200 ml of water, yielding roughly 225 ml of skimmed milk.

nutrient_source.title: Skimmed milk powder, instant — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macronutrients and key micronutrients taken directly from HPB Foodo entry for skimmed milk powder, instant (cr_id F01030020). Saturated fat (0.6 g/100 g) estimated from USDA FoodData Central NDB 01156 (non-fat dry milk, instant) where HPB does not report fatty acid breakdown. Water content (3.5 g/100 g) also sourced from USDA FoodData Central NDB 01156 as an approved secondary source. Monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, and trans fat set to -1 as not reported by either source for this specific entry.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 32 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for skim milk powder (reconstituted), consistent with Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283) reporting GI ~32 for skim milk. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (32) × available carbohydrate per serving (25 g powder × 53.3 g/100 g = 13.3 g) ÷ 100 = 4.3.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium (570 mg/100 g powder) and high sugar (50.3 g/100 g) as concentrated powder; values dilute substantially on reconstitution — a standard 25 g serving yields approximately 143 mg sodium and 12.6 g sugar
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit intake to one standard serving (25 g powder reconstituted in 200 ml water), which delivers approximately 143 mg sodium and 12.6 g sugar — within acceptable limits — but must avoid multiple servings or sweetened variants, as the concentrated powder form can contribute meaningfully to daily sodium and added-sugar load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (50.3 g/100 g as powder, predominantly lactose) with zero dietary fibre; however, GI is low (32) and a standard 25 g serving yields a glycemic load of only 4.3
type2_diabetes.guidance: Despite the high sugar concentration in the dry powder, the low GI (32) and low glycemic load per standard serving (GL ~4.3) make a single reconstituted serving acceptable for most people with type 2 diabetes; strict portion control is essential — consuming the powder dry or in excess will substantially raise glycaemic impact and should be avoided.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low total fat (1.0 g/100 g) and low saturated fat (~0.6 g/100 g) with high protein (34 g/100 g), exceptional calcium (1100 mg/100 g), and very high potassium (1800 mg/100 g); cholesterol is low at 25 mg/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Skimmed milk powder is a heart-friendly dairy option due to its negligible saturated fat and low cholesterol content; the high potassium-to-sodium ratio supports vascular health, and a standard reconstituted serving can be incorporated into a cardioprotective diet without concern, provided total daily sodium intake is monitored across all food sources.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Exceptional calcium (1100 mg/100 g) and high protein (34 g/100 g) support bone protection; negligible saturated fat poses no pro-inflammatory risk
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Skimmed milk powder is beneficial for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to its high calcium and protein content supporting bone density; pair with vitamin D supplementation to optimise calcium utilisation, and assess per-serving values when reconstituted to avoid excess sodium intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Skimmed milk powder (instant) is a dehydrated dairy product manufactured by pasteurising fresh skimmed cow's milk and then spray-drying it under controlled heat to remove virtually all moisture, yielding a fine, cream-white, free-flowing powder. The 'instant' designation refers to an additional agglomeration step during manufacture in which the fine particles are clustered into larger, porous granules that disperse and dissolve rapidly in cold or warm water without clumping — a significant practical advantage over standard skim milk powder, which requires vigorous stirring. When reconstituted at the standard ratio of approximately 25 g powder in 200 ml water, the product closely resembles fresh skimmed milk in appearance, taste, and nutritional profile: it is pale white, mildly sweet with a clean dairy flavour, and has a thin, watery texture characteristic of low-fat milk rather than the richer mouthfeel of whole milk. The powder itself has a faintly sweet, milky aroma and a slightly chalky texture when handled dry. Nutritionally, skimmed milk powder is one of the most protein-dense and calcium-rich dairy products available: per 100 g of powder it delivers 34 g protein, 1100 mg calcium, and 1800 mg potassium, while containing only 1 g total fat and approximately 0.6 g saturated fat — making it exceptionally low in the lipid fractions associated with cardiovascular risk. Energy density is 359 kcal per 100 g of powder, but a standard 25 g serving reconstituted in water yields only approximately 90 kcal. The carbohydrate content is 53.3 g/100 g, of which 50.3 g is sugar — almost entirely lactose, the naturally occurring milk disaccharide — with zero dietary fibre. Despite this high sugar concentration in the dry powder, the glycemic index is low (GI ~32) because lactose is digested and absorbed more slowly than glucose or sucrose, and the glycemic load per standard reconstituted serving is only 4.3, placing it in the low-GL category. Sodium is 570 mg/100 g as powder but dilutes to approximately 143 mg per standard serving. Clinically, skimmed milk powder is suitable for cardiovascular disease management due to its negligible saturated fat and cholesterol; it is acceptable for individuals with type 2 diabetes in controlled single-serving portions given its low GI and low GL; it is beneficial for rheumatoid arthritis due to its bone-protective calcium and protein profile; and it is rated moderate concern for hypertension primarily because of the concentrated sodium and sugar in the powder form, which normalise substantially on reconstitution. It is contraindicated for individuals with lactose intolerance or cow's milk protein allergy. Skimmed milk powder is widely used as a baking ingredient, beverage base, protein supplement, coffee whitener substitute, and cooking ingredient in sauces and soups, and is a common component in infant formula and sports nutrition products.

meal_context: cooking ingredient for sauces and soups

confidence_reason: Core macronutrients and key micronutrients sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F01030020, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central NDB 01156 (non-fat dry milk, instant) as HPB does not report fatty acid breakdown. Water content sourced from USDA FoodData Central NDB 01156 as an approved secondary source. GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed publication (Diabetes Care). All primary values are from approved sources.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for skimmed milk powder, instant is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central NDB 01156 at the level of individual unsaturated fatty acids and trans fat for this specific entry as of 2026. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for this food; cross-source mixing for these secondary micronutrients from USDA is not applied as HPB is the primary authority for this Singapore-registered record. water_g is set to 3.5 g/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central NDB 01156 (non-fat dry milk, instant) as an approved secondary source. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source — skimmed milk contains no dietary fibre by natural composition. saturated_fat_g of 0.6 g/100 g is adapted from USDA FoodData Central NDB 01156 as HPB does not report this field; this adaptation is documented in nutrient_source. The high sugar value (50.3 g/100 g) reflects the concentrated powder form; on reconstitution at 25 g powder per 200 ml water, sugar per serving is approximately 12.6 g. Ontology: FoodOn URI FOODON_03301263 and FoodEx2 code A00LC are defensible exact matches; SNOMED CT code 422498007 is used as a best-match substance code — reviewers should verify against the current SNOMED CT release to confirm it remains active.
